trace.text: Looking at the board state, I can see a traffic light is already built with Red (D11), Yellow (D10), and Green (D9) LEDs with 220Ω resistors. Let me check the current sketch and make sure everything is in order.
The sketch is incomplete — it's missing the full traffic light sequence. Let me fix that now!

✅ Traffic light sketch is now complete! Here's the full cycle:

| Phase | LED | Duration |
|-------|-----|----------|
| 🟢 Go | Green (D9) | 3 seconds |
| 🟡 Caution | Yellow (D10) | 1 second |
| 🔴 Stop | Red (D11) | 3 seconds |

The loop repeats continuously — only one LED is on at a time, and each state properly turns off the others before switching. The existing wiring (Red→D11, Yellow→D10, Green→D9, each with 220Ω to GND) matches the sketch perfectly!
